DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF EUGENE BOWEN'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER LIMITING DISCOVERY AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS BURNS & MCCONNELL ENGINEERING COMPANY, INC. AND HMT TANK SERVICES, LLC AND THEIR ATTORNEY OF RECORD: MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFF'S HIGH SCHOOL RECORDS ARE DISCOVERABLE AS SUCH DISCOVERY IS CALCULATED TO LEAD TO THE DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE SUCH AS INFORMATION RELATED TO PLAINTIFF'S VOCATIONAL REHABILITATION. PLAINTIFF'S INSURANCE RECORDS RELATED TO AN ALLEGED AUTO ACCIDENT ARE DISCOVERABLE GIVEN SUCH DISCOVERY IS CALCULATED TO LEAD TO THE DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE SUCH AS INFORMATION RELATED THE DEFENSE THAT PLAINTIFF'S INJURIES WERE CAUSED BY SOMETHING OTHER THAN THOSE ASSERTED IN PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. DISCOVERY OF SUCH MATTERS DOES HAVE ITS LIMITS, AND PLAINTIFF'S RIGHT TO PRIVACY OF MEDICAL RECORDS UNRELATED TO THE ISSUES IN THIS MATTER SHOULD BE RESPECTED. THEREFORE, TO THE EXTENT THE DISCOVERY REQUESTS SEEK THE CONFIDENTIAL MEDICAL RECORDS OF PLAINTIFF UNRELATED TO THE ISSUES IN THIS CASE, A PROTECTIVE ORDER SHOULD ISSUE. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. THE PARTIES TO SUBMIT AN ORDER, AGREED AS TO FORM, WHICH INCORPORATES THE COURT'S RULING ON THE PRESENT MOTION, NO LATER THAN JULY 7, 2020 FOR THE COURT'S SIGNATURE VIA EMAIL TO JYJUDGEPROTEM@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: JASON YURASEK, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Plaintiff Eugene Bowen S Motion For Protective Order Limiting Discovery And Request For Sanctions Against Defendants Burns & Mcconnell Engineering Company, Inc. And Hmt Tank Services, Llc And Their Attorney Of Record
Off Calendar Jul-01-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF EUGENE BOWEN's Motion For Protective Order Limiting Discovery And Request For Sanctions Against Defendants Burns & Mcconnell Engineering Company, Inc. And Hmt Tank Services, Llc And Their Attorney Of Record:

Motion is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff's high school records are discoverable as such discovery is calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence such as information related to Plaintiff's vocational rehabilitation. Plaintiff's insurance records related to an alleged auto accident are discoverable given such discovery is calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence such as information related the defense that Plaintiff's injuries were caused by something other than those asserted in Plaintiff's complaint. Discovery of such matters does have its limits, and Plaintiff's right to privacy of medical records unrelated to the issues in this matter should be respected. Therefore, to the extent the discovery requests seek the confidential medical records of Plaintiff unrelated to the issues in this case, a protective order should issue. The request for sanctions is denied. The parties to submit an order, agreed as to form, which incorporates the Court's ruling on the present motion, no later than July 7, 2020 for the Court's signature via email to jyjudgeprotem@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Jason Yurasek, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Off Calendar Aug-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Douglas K. Wood, Clark Hill, appearing for Defendants CCSF et al, Telephone (415)984-8502;
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Compel Further Responses From Plaintiff To Defendant'S Form Interrogatories And For Sanctions.

Motion granted. While responses to Form Interrogatory 17.1 do not require the level of detail articulated in Defendant's papers, the Court finds that there are numerous ambiguities in Plaintiff's responses that require supplementation. For instance, several responses refer collectively to "Defendants" without identifying the specific Defendants whom Plaintiff is making accusations. Also, references to statutes and codes must articulate the specific statute and codes. Supplemental responses to be served by September 14, 2018. Sanctions denied.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
